h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: Three tiles to the bar line; that is the top box doing the counting.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Then the bar is three eighth-note beats, not three generic places.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply continue using the tiles as generic beat counters while swapping the beat-unit card to change the time signature.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: sets three blank tiles up to the bar line, puts the eighth-note card in the lower box, and draws an eighth note on each tile
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully enacts the new representational device by separating the counting function from the note-value function on the physical tiles.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The tile route has stopped settling it because the tile was carrying two jobs. Use the blank side for beat places only: keep three places before the bar line, put the eighth-note card on the lower box, then draw the note shape each blank place is now claiming.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly names the failure of the prior route and introduces a concrete physical modification to separate the two cognitive jobs.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Use the blank side for beat places only: keep three places before the bar line, put the eighth-note card on the lower box, then draw the note shape each blank place is now claiming.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The device is highly specific, directly resolves the representational collision the learner identified, and gives a clear, actionable physical constraint.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Blank tile first marks the count; the lower box gives each counted place its note value.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor directly addresses the learner's final question about whether the tile still only marks the count by explicitly synthesizing the dual function of the new blank-tile mechanism.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor explicitly names the failure of the prior route and introduces a concrete physical modification to separate the two cognitive jobs.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The tile route has stopped settling it because the tile was carrying two jobs. Use the blank side for beat places only: keep three places before the bar line, put the eighth-note card on the lower box, then draw the note shape each blank place is now claiming.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor explicitly names the failure of the prior route and introduces a concrete physical modification to separate the two cognitive jobs.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The device is highly specific, directly resolves the representational collision the learner identified, and gives a clear, actionable physical constraint.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue placing cards and assigning arrow directions according to the newly established 'other object first' rule.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: If this card is also supposed to cover rubbing, that needs a separate sideways arrow.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner independently generates a new representational criterion, recognizing that a single arrow cannot simultaneously represent normal force and friction, thus advancing the problem space.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The arrowhead is still trying to read as cart motion. This strip stays down until the sentence passes one test: other object on cart.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly acknowledges the failure of the current visual setup and introduces a physical occlusion mechanism to force the learner to separate object identification from direction assignment.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: This strip stays down until the sentence passes one test: other object on cart. Put track in the slot. Say: track on cart. Then lift the strip and choose the direction for that named interaction.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The blank strip is a highly specific, actionable constraint that directly neutralizes the visual bias the learner identified, perfectly fitted to the live pressure.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The arrowhead is still trying to read as cart motion. This strip stays down until the sentence passes one test: other object on cart.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor visibly changes the physical task and evidence standard by introducing the occlusion strip in direct response to the learner's observation about the arrow's visual bias.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: If this card is also supposed to cover rubbing, that needs a separate sideways arrow.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner independently generates a new representational criterion, recognizing that a single arrow cannot simultaneously represent normal force and friction, thus advancing the problem space.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: The arrowhead is still trying to read as cart motion. This strip stays down until the sentence passes one test: other object on cart.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor visibly changes the physical task and evidence standard by introducing the occlusion strip in direct response to the learner's observation about the arrow's visual bias.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The arrowhead is still trying to read as cart motion. This strip stays down until the sentence passes one test: other object on cart.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor explicitly acknowledges the failure of the current visual setup and introduces a physical occlusion mechanism to force the learner to separate object identification from direction assignment.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: The arrowhead is still trying to read as cart motion. This strip stays down until the sentence passes one test: other object on cart.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor explicitly acknowledges the failure of the current visual setup and introduces a physical occlusion mechanism to force the learner to separate object identification from direction assignment.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.evidence: This strip stays down until the sentence passes one test: other object on cart. Put track in the slot. Say: track on cart. Then lift the strip and choose the direction for that named interaction.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The blank strip is a highly specific, actionable constraint that directly neutralizes the visual bias the learner identified, perfectly fitted to the live pressure.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner attempts to refine the metadata note to distinguish between a single row value and a column-wide claim.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Slot A. Release-note sentence: `rainfall_mm` is the field label for the rainfall amount, in millimeters, recorded on a city-month row.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully applies the newly introduced Slot A/B classification and release-note gate to formulate a precise sentence, showing actional progress despite lingering conceptual shakiness.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The `42` check settled the value; it did not settle the header. Switch to the release-note gate: put `rainfall_mm` in one slot only. Slot A: field label, naming the kind of value a row records in that column. Slot B: column claim, asserting a result about the column’s rows.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor explicitly contrasts the failed prior route with a new structural device (Slot A vs Slot B release-note gate) to force a categorical distinction.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Switch to the release-note gate: put `rainfall_mm` in one slot only. Slot A: field label, naming the kind of value a row records in that column. Slot B: column claim, asserting a result about the column’s rows. Mark A or B, then write one release-note sentence a data user could not mistake for Slot B.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is highly specific, introduces a clear binary constraint, and gives the learner an actionable writing task that directly targets the conceptual blockage.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn demonstrating a revised framing, so no recognition-contingent uptake can occur.

Slot B: column claim, asserting a result about the column’s rows. Mark A or B, then write one release-note sentence a data user could not mistake for Slot B.
